page: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor, delivering 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The CPU includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities and memory bandwidth. The instance achieved top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, Passmark CPU score, and Redis SET operations, which reached 114.69 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput also placed in the top tier at 105.74 GB/sec. Large-block memory read bandwidth is strong at 197.6 GB/sec, though large-block memory write bandwidth is average at 79.1 GB/sec. For machine learning tasks, the hardware-assisted AMX instructions support top-tier prompt processing speeds for 7B and 70B LLM models, though text generation for large 70B models drops to an average tier of 3.52 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8i-flex.16xlarge offers a high density of modern DDR5 memory and dedicated compute resources, making it a balanced option for workloads requiring high memory bandwidth and integer performance without the premium of specialized memory-optimized instances. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy operations. This server is suited for tasks such as static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of 7 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier performance in Geekbench, Passmark CPU, and Redis SET benchmarks, alongside strong multi-core and memory read capabilities. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which accelerate LLM prompt processing. This instance is suited for workloads such as static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It delivers top-tier Redis and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.9 GHz processor.
tagline: A balanced 64-vCPU general-purpose instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and advanced Intel matrix acceleration for compute-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores via Intel Xeon 6975P-C at 3.9 GHz
256.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory (4.0 GB per core)
Top-tier performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated LLM prompt processing
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
